title: Nonograms
intro: Nonograms is a logic puzzle in which the goal is to place black cells such that number constraints found on the edges of the board are satisfied.
rule_hints: To the left of each row and on top of each column are numbers that show the lengths of runs of consecutive black squares for that row/column.
goal: Your goal is to mark cells black to create runs that match these numbers.
example_column: For instance, in the example below, looking at the right column with hint "3", each of the 2 shown placements satisfies the number clue since each consists of 3 consecutive black squares.
example_row: Similarly, the bottom row with hints "1" and "2" can be satisfied in multiple ways - a single black cell, then at least one empty cell, then 2 consecutive black cells.
rule_multiple_runs: Note that when there are multiple runs in the same row/column, they must be separated by at least one empty cell.
solved: The correct solution will uniquely satisfy the runs specified by all rows and column hints simultaneously
solved_board: Below is a full solved game board:
control_cycle: Left click to cycle forward, right click to cycle backward between:

compact.rule_runs: Each row and column has numbers showing the lengths of runs of consecutive black cells.
compact.rule_mark: Mark cells black () to create runs matching those lengths, in the given order.
compact.rule_separation: Runs must be separated with at least one white/empty cell ().
